name: HazardHunterPH / GeoRiskPH
access_method: web application and public GeoRiskPH surfaces where allowed
mvp_use: reference official hazard categories and compare against machine-readable layers
automation_status: manual-first until API terms and endpoints are validated
notes: Do not bypass access controls. Use as official context and validate any automatable endpoint separately.

name: Project NOAH Hazard Maps on BetterGov
automation_status: candidate for MVP after resource download validation
notes: Validate license, resource URLs, vector-tile or download format, and layer schema before scoring.

name: PHIVOLCS FaultFinder
access_method: web application and documented PHIVOLCS services where allowed
mvp_use: nearest active-fault distance or manual official cross-check
automation_status: manual-first until service terms and query method are validated
notes: Use for proximity to active faults. Do not treat as structural safety certification.

name: PHIVOLCS Liquefaction MapServer
mvp_use: point-in-polygon or map-layer query for liquefaction exposure
automation_status: candidate for MVP after layer query validation
notes: Record PHIVOLCS copyright and layer vintage in outputs.

name: MMDA Metro Manila GIS Hub
role: local Metro Manila risk and base geography
mvp_use: city boundary joins, flood-prone point distance, river proximity
notes: Validated flood-prone points, rivers, and city boundaries in the source deep dive.

name: HDX Philippines GeoHazards Data
mvp_use: fallback hazard geometry or cross-check against PHIVOLCS/GeoRiskPH
automation_status: candidate after file format and vintage validation
notes: Some resources may be KMZ or imagery rather than clean vector layers.

name: OpenStreetMap / Geofabrik Philippines
role: waterway, road, facility, and weak-service proxy source
access_method: Geofabrik PBF/GPKG downloads and lightweight Overpass checks
mvp_use: waterway proximity, access context, waste/disposal proxy only when tags are present
automation_status: MVP-ready for bulk ETL with ODbL handling
notes: ODbL attribution required. Do not treat missing OSM tags as absence of risk.
